description.en: Resolve a name to an object. This functionality is used in numerous places in the standard library (see bpo-12915) - and equivalent functionality is also in widely used third-party packages such as setuptools, Django and Pyramid. It is expected that name will be a string in one of the following formats, where W is shorthand for a valid Python identifier and dot stands for a literal period in these pseudo-regexes: W(.W)* W(.W)*:(W(.W)*)? The first form is intended for backward compatibility on...
